title: lupinus albus var. graecus
intro: Lupinus albus var. graecus is a variety of the white lupin, a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's medicinal properties are derived from its seeds and pods, which contain alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, the plant has been used to support kidney and urinary health, as well as to treat conditions such as gout and arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Lupinus albus var. graecus can be consumed as a tea or used in tinctures, and is also sometimes used as a food source, particularly in traditional Greek cuisine.
